title: commelina bracteosa subsp. rhizomifera
intro: Commelina bracteosa subsp. rhizomifera is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae. It is a type of dayflower, known for its distinctive blue-purple flowers and long, thin leaves. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, with its rhizomes containing compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. In traditional medicine, Commelina bracteosa subsp. rhizomifera is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or poultice for its herbal benefits.
